name: Krishna GSVV
title: I'm Krishna GSVV — developer, researcher & hackathon champion
bio: I'm a software engineer and AI researcher who builds at the intersection of technology and real-world impact. What started as curiosity about how computers work has grown into published research, hackathon wins, and a growing library of open-source tools that people actually use. I specialize in full-stack development with React and Python, and I dive deep into AI whenever I get the chance. Right now I'm building EquiLens — a platform to detect bias in language models — alongside computer vision apps and blockchain tools like HiveXplore. Whether it's a VS Code extension or an ML pipeline, I care about code that's clean, fast, and worth using. I also contribute actively to AI research — my work on diabetic retinopathy prediction using deep learning is published on IEEE, and the EquiLens corpus dataset is released on Zenodo. On the competition front, I've won ₹3.75L+ across hackathons at IIT Delhi, IIT Hyderabad, Tredence Inc., and Microsoft. I believe technology should be accessible and transparent. My open-source portfolio — 25+ projects including VS Code extensions with 2K+ downloads — reflects that belief. Always building, always learning, always looking for the next interesting problem.
